articles.title: Waltham animal control officers recover ‘pile ’o pigs’ dumped in woods, cemetery
articles.description:
Animal control officers recovered 19 guinea pigs, including 10 found at the cemetery and nine found in the woods.

The post Waltham animal control officers recover ‘pile ’o pigs’ dumped in woods, cemetery appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Animal control officers recovered 19 guinea pigs dumped in Waltham woods and a cemetery.
Ten guinea pigs were found at the cemetery, nine in the woods.
Local residents are urged to report any animal dumping to prevent similar incidents.

articles.title: What Drake Maye, Milton Williams said of A.J. Brown’s potential arrival in New England
articles.description:
"If he ends up being on our team, great. What a great player. And if he doesn’t, we’ve still got to work these guys here."

summary: Drake Maye and Milton Williams discussed A.J. Brown's potential impact on the Patriots.
A.J. Brown is a top player, enhancing the team's offensive options if acquired.
Boston residents may see increased excitement for the Patriots' upcoming season with potential trades.

articles.title: Something fishy? Large groups of dead fish spotted in Boston, Worcester ponds
articles.description:
These groups of dead fish may make for an odd sight, but officials say it’s actually part of a natural seasonal event.

The post Something fishy? Large groups of dead fish spotted in Boston, Worcester ponds appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Officials report large groups of dead fish in Boston and Worcester ponds.
This phenomenon is part of a natural seasonal event, not pollution.
Residents are advised not to be alarmed as it's expected to occur annually.

articles.title: N.H. man killed his mother in murder-suicide, officials say
articles.description:
Autopsy results released on Wednesday confirm Eileen Shevelin, 78, was killed by her son Thomas Wallace, 57, in a murder-suicide,

The post N.H. man killed his mother in murder-suicide, officials say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Thomas Wallace, 57, killed his mother Eileen Shevelin, 78, in a murder-suicide.
Autopsy results confirmed the incident occurred in New Hampshire, affecting nearby Boston communities.
This tragedy raises concerns about mental health and domestic violence awareness in the region.

articles.title: East Boston couple facing civil rights charges after alleged racist attack on group dining at restaurant
articles.description:
The couple allegedly threw a piece of wood at a group of Hispanic diners, sprayed them with water, and called them several racist slurs.

The post East Boston couple facing civil rights charges after alleged racist attack on group dining at restaurant appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: East Boston couple charged after allegedly attacking Hispanic diners at a restaurant.
Incident involved throwing wood, spraying water, and using racist slurs against the group.
Charges highlight ongoing issues of racism and civil rights in Boston's dining establishments.

articles.title: What Craig Breslow said about the Red Sox potentially selling off pieces amid continued struggles
articles.description:

summary: Craig Breslow emphasizes commitment to improving the Red Sox's performance this season.

Potential trades could reshape the roster, affecting local support and future team dynamics.

articles.title: Fall River police looking for man accused of destroying shooting victim’s memorial
articles.description:
“We included the video with the expletives edited out although it does not take a literary scholar to fill in the blanks,” police said.

summary: Fall River police seek a suspect for vandalizing a shooting victim's memorial.
The incident involved a memorial for a shooting victim, details not disclosed.
Community members express outrage over the destruction of the memorial.
